CheckTitle: Detect service principals with both password and certificate credentials
Description: Checks for service principals that have both password (secret) and certificate (key) credentials. Best practice is to use only certificates. Having both types suggests incomplete migration from secrets to certificates.
Risk: Service principals with both credential types have an expanded attack surface. Password credentials are weaker and more easily exfiltrated than certificates. If the intent is to use certificates only, leftover password credentials provide an alternative authentication path for attackers.

Remediation.Recommendation: Remove password credentials from service principals that also have certificate credentials. Complete the migration to certificate-only authentication.

SourceNotes: Certificate-only authentication is the recommended practice.
